categories.summary: Clean SDK integration with proper typing and error boundaries.
Preview mode works but lacks live editing support for inline content.
Well-structured component hierarchy with clear data flow patterns.
API keys exposed in client bundle; missing CSP headers.
Several packages 2+ major versions behind; no automated update strategy.
Vercel deployment with proper build caching and environment separation.

topRisks.title: API key exposed in client-side bundle
topRisks.businessContext: Leaked keys allow unauthorized API access and potential data exfiltration.

topRisks.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy headers
topRisks.businessContext: XSS attacks could inject malicious scripts without CSP protection.

topRisks.title: React 17 with known hydration vulnerabilities
topRisks.businessContext: Unpatched framework exposes users to documented attack vectors.

result.briefMarkdown:
# Audit Report: acme/ecommerce-storefront

## Executive Summary

The storefront is architecturally sound with clean CMS integration but has critical security gaps that must be addressed before the next production deployment.

## Key Findings

- **Critical:** API delivery key is bundled into the client JavaScript, exposing it to any visitor
- **High:** No Content-Security-Policy headers configured, leaving the app vulnerable to XSS
- **High:** React 17.0.2 has known hydration vulnerabilities patched in 18.x
- Several dependencies are 2+ major versions behind with no Dependabot or Renovate configured

## Recommendations

1. Move API keys to server-side routes or middleware (immediate)
2. Add CSP headers via next.config.js security headers (this sprint)
3. Plan React 18 migration — hydration fixes alone justify the upgrade
4. Enable Dependabot for automated dependency PRs

findings.title: API key exposed in client-side bundle
findings.description: The Sitecore delivery API key is referenced directly in a client component without the NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix guard, causing it to be bundled into the browser JavaScript.

evidence.description: API key accessed in a module imported by client components

findings.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy headers
findings.description: No CSP headers are configured in next.config.js or middleware, leaving the application vulnerable to cross-site scripting attacks.

evidence.description: next.config.js has no security headers configuration

findings.title: React 17 with known hydration vulnerabilities
findings.description: The project uses React 17.0.2 which has documented hydration mismatch vulnerabilities that can be exploited for XSS in specific conditions.

evidence.description: Outdated React version with known security patches in 18.x

findings.title: Live editing not supported for inline text fields
findings.description: Preview mode renders correctly but inline text editing falls back to a full page reload instead of using the Experience Editor bridge.

evidence.description: Missing Sitecore editable wrapper for inline editing support

findings.title: No automated dependency update strategy
findings.description: Neither Dependabot nor Renovate is configured. 8 packages are 2+ major versions behind their latest release.

evidence.description: No dependabot.yml or renovate.json found in repository

findings.title: Prop drilling through 4+ component levels
findings.description: Layout context data is passed through 4 intermediate components before reaching leaf nodes. Consider React Context or a state management solution.

evidence.snippet: export const MainLayout = ({ navigation, footer, seo, children, sitecoreContext }: Props) => (
evidence.description: Layout passes 5 props that are consumed 4 levels deep

findings.title: Clean component factory pattern with type safety
findings.description: The component registry uses a typed factory pattern that maps Sitecore rendering names to React components with full TypeScript coverage.

evidence.snippet:
import { ComponentFactory } from '@sitecore-jss/sitecore-jss-nextjs';

const components = new Map<string, React.ComponentType>();
evidence.description: Well-implemented component factory with type-safe registration
